core_mechanism: An age-structured (elapsed-time-indexed) compartmental population-flow model in which the probability of transitioning between states is a dose-response function of the accumulated input received from interacting agents — i.e., a structured renewal process with load-dependent transition kernels and an intermediate buffer state between exposure and full transition.
brief_reason: The abstract constructs a compartmental spreading model with a discrete age-of-infection structure that implicitly tracks accumulated load and makes the probability of transitioning to the infected state a function of the load received from contacts, which is exactly an elapsed-time-structured compartmental flow with dose-dependent transition kernels.
